groups.explanation: The harness correctly writes fuzz bytes to a temp file and opens it read-only with H5Fopen — valid API usage with no precondition violations. The OOM occurs inside H5C__load_entry (H5Centry.c:1155) where actual_len (~4 GB) is derived from file-controlled metadata via get_final_load_size. The only guard is H5C__verify_len_eoa, but the fuzzer can craft matching EOA metadata so that check passes, and H5MM_realloc is called with the unreasonable size. HDF5 needs an absolute sanity cap on actual_len before allocating.
groups.suggestedFixDirection: In H5C__load_entry (H5Centry.c, after the H5C__verify_len_eoa call), add a hard upper-bound check on actual_len (e.g., reject values exceeding a few hundred MB or a configurable cache maximum) before calling H5MM_realloc, so that a crafted file cannot trigger an unbounded allocation during speculative superblock loading.
